title: Pandemic Supply-Tree Stockpile: Safety-Stock Placement under a Fill-Rate Constraint
strategies: uniform split: divide the stockpile evenly across depot + every district, ignoring how different their demand is (baseline)
demand-proportional local sizing: allocate to each district in proportion to its mean demand, no safety buffer or central reserve

distribution-driven marginal allocation: draw own Monte-Carlo demand from the public mean/std and add stock one unit at a time where expected (holding+escalation+shortage) cost drops most, discovering per-district buffers and central pooling reserves
explicit newsvendor buffers per district plus a variance-scaled depot/internal-node pooling reserve tuned to the escalation-vs-shortage trade-off
